mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration and impending recessionary pressures, causing investors to de-risk and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising real auto sales and declining federal funds rates indicates robust consumer spending supported by cheap credit, which boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no statistical significance in predicting 20-day returns, or positive returns fail to materialize when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which reduces input cost pressures and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5-day returns during recovery regimes, or the signal reverses direction when supply chains are constrained.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and higher aggregate income, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 240, it signals entrenched inflation that forces aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240, or the threshold effect disappears in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising housing starts and falling unemployment reflects synchronized strength in the housing and labor markets, driving consumer confidence and capital expenditure that produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual proxies, or 20-day returns are negative when both housing starts rise and unemployment falls.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth during early-cycle expansions signals broad-based economic recovery and rising corporate profit margins, which reliably generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth loses predictive power for 60-day returns outside early-cycle phases, or the signal predicts negative returns during late-cycle expansions.
